text: Four laparoscopic ports are placed on the left side of the abdomen. The transverse colon is retracted upwards, exposing the third part of the duodenum. The surgeon divides the overlying peritoneum and mobilizes the duodenum.

Four laparoscopic ports are placed on the left side of the abdomen. The transverse colon is retracted upwards, exposing the third part of the duodenum. The surgeon divides the overlying peritoneum and mobilizes the duodenum.

The surgeon identifies the ligament of Treitz and positions a segment of the proximal jejunum parallel to the duodenum. Two stay sutures of 3-0 Vicryl are placed to align the jejunum and duodenum together.

The surgeon identifies the ligament of Treitz and positions a segment of the proximal jejunum parallel to the duodenum. Two stay sutures of 3-0 Vicryl are used to align the jejunum and duodenum together.

Enterotomies are created in both the duodenum and jejunum segments. A side-to-side duodenal jejunostomy is constructed using a 45mm linear stapler with a white load cartridge. The surgeon inspects the inside of the anastomosis for bleeding. The enterotomy is closed with a running suture of Tuo polyester.

The surgeon closes the mesenteric defect using several interrupted polyester sutures, ensuring the defect is securely sealed.
